The '''Naboo Royal Lifter''' was a troop transport spaceship employed by the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]].

==Characteristics==
The Naboo Royal Lifter was an unarmed [[Starship/Legends|starship]] used to [[Transport/Legends|transport]] troops and [[Droid/Legends|droid]] workers across the battlefield. The bow of the vessel was [[Chromium/Legends|chromium]], and the rest of the vessel was [[Color/Legends|colored]] yellow. The main body was shaped like a teardrop and featured two stubby wings. They were produced at [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Security Force]] airbases. The Royal Lifter's capacity was five troops, even if the troops were mounted.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
Between [[39 BBY/Legends|39]] and [[36 BBY/Legends|36 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> one was used to transport [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] to [[Alaris Prime/Legends|Alaris Prime]] in the [[Battle for Alaris Prime]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Naboo Royal Lifter appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. In the last mission of the tutorial, one Royal Lifter waits on the edge of the map for the player to build a Jedi Temple. If done so, it delivers Jinn to join in the battle. This article assumes the player did so. Royal Lifters were the Naboo civilization's "air transport" unit, and cost 29 units of food and 138 units of [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]]. The campaign was later included in the [[2002]] [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|expansion pack]]. A picture of it is used to represent the "air transport" unit in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]''.
